title: Last Sunday after Pentecost: Christ the King
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
November 24, 2019

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is Christ the King Sunday, the final Sunday of Ordinary Time and the last Sunday after Pentecost, occurring one week before the First Sunday of Advent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings would have been Ezekiel 34:11-16, 20-24 (God as shepherd king), Psalm 100 (jubilant thanksgiving), Ephesians 1:15-23 (Paul's prayer about Christ's authority), and Matthew 25:31-46 (the final judgment and separation of sheep from goats). The sermon would have emphasized Christ's kingship and sovereignty as the church year prepared to transition into the Advent season.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of denominational tension following the 2018 General Convention's decisions on marriage and sexuality. The Diocese of Colorado, led by Bishop Robert Wright, was navigating these national liturgical and theological shifts. Saint John's Cathedral had been undergoing its own internal discussions about inclusion and pastoral care in the post-2018 landscape. No major diocesan elections or crises were imminent at this specific moment.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver had just experienced the Thanksgiving holiday week with typical late November weather patterns (temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit, clear mountain region skies). The Denver Broncos were mid-season at 5-6 record, with local sports media focused on coaching changes and quarterback questions heading into December. The Colorado legislature was out of session, though Mayor Michael Hancock's administration was managing typical November municipal affairs. No major weather events or community crises marked the week immediately preceding November 24.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS

The impeachment inquiry into President Trump dominated headlines, with House Intelligence Committee public hearings ongoing throughout the week of November 18-24, featuring testimony from State Department officials regarding the Ukraine scandal. The 2020 Democratic primary was intensifying with candidates competing heavily in early states, particularly ahead of the Iowa caucuses two months away. The labor movement was active, with various strikes and negotiations ongoing in automotive, film, and other industries, though no single dominant strike characterized this specific week.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Hong Kong protests, which had been escalating since spring 2019, were reaching a critical moment with clashes at universities, including the Chinese University of Hong Kong being occupied by demonstrators. The Syrian civil war continued with Russian and Turkish military involvement, and discussions were underway about potential political settlements. Climate change remained a global concern with environmental reports dominating coverage as COP25 (the UN Climate Change Conference) was beginning in Madrid the week after this sermon date.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a pre-holiday cultural moment marked by anxious political division over impeachment proceedings, creating a tense national mood heading into Thanksgiving and toward Christmas. "The Crown" Season 3 had just been released on Netflix (November 17), sparking cultural conversations about the British royal family and institutional authority. Popular culture reflected concerns about institutional trust, individual conscience, and questions of power and justice—themes that would have resonated with the Christ the King emphasis on ultimate sovereignty beyond earthly kingdoms.
